name: Untether AI
summary: Toronto-based startup built energy-efficient at-memory AI inference chips but failed to pivot fast enough to the generative-AI hardware boom.
lesson: Being early to an efficient-compute thesis doesn't matter if you can't ship product before the market's needs shift underneath you.

name: Esperanto Technologies
summary: Built a 1,000-core RISC-V AI accelerator chip but landed too few customers after years of development and wound down its silicon business.
lesson: A technically impressive chip with no anchor customer cannot outrun the cash burn of custom silicon development.

name: Airspan Networks
summary: 5G and Open RAN hardware vendor filed prepackaged Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2024 after Open RAN adoption grew far slower than expected.
lesson: Building capacity for a market inflection point that arrives years late can bankrupt a company waiting for it.

name: Pareteum Corporation
summary: Telecom connectivity software firm inflated revenue by over $40M from non-binding purchase orders; executives were later criminally charged and the company collapsed into Chapter 11.
lesson: Recognizing revenue on commitments customers never actually made is fraud, and it eventually destroys the company it was meant to prop up.

name: OneWeb
summary: LEO satellite broadband constellation builder filed Chapter 11 in March 2020 when a SoftBank-led funding round collapsed amid COVID-19 market turmoil; later rescued and merged into Eutelsat.
lesson: Capital-intensive satellite constellations need financing committed well before a downturn, not mid-buildout when investors can walk away.

name: LightSquared
summary: Attempted to build a hybrid satellite-terrestrial LTE network but the FCC blocked deployment after tests showed severe interference with GPS receivers, forcing Chapter 11.
lesson: Spectrum plans that interfere with critical safety infrastructure like GPS will be blocked no matter how much capital has already been spent.

name: Clearwire
summary: Built a nationwide WiMAX-based 4G wireless network, burning over $10 billion, before being acquired by Sprint in 2013 having never reached profitability.
lesson: Betting an entire national network build on a wireless standard that loses the industry standards war is an existential risk.

name: Sycamore Networks
summary: Optical networking equipment maker was one of the dot-com era's most extreme boom-bust stories, falling from a peak market cap near $45B to near zero before winding down.
lesson: Valuations built on the assumption that internet bandwidth demand would grow indefinitely can evaporate the moment the buildout cycle pauses.

name: Virgin Orbit
summary: Air-launched small satellite rocket company filed Chapter 11 in April 2023 after a failed UK launch and inability to raise a bridge round.
lesson: A single high-profile launch failure can trigger a funding collapse when a company is already burning cash faster than it launches.

name: Astra Space
summary: Small satellite launch and spacecraft engine company came close to bankruptcy after repeated launch failures and cash shortfalls, ultimately taken private in 2024.
lesson: Rushing a minimum-viable rocket to market to hit investor timelines multiplies both technical and financial risk.

name: Habana Labs
summary: Israeli AI training and inference chip startup was acquired by Intel for roughly $2 billion in 2019, becoming the foundation of Intel's Gaudi AI accelerator line.
lesson: Building a credible alternative to Nvidia early in the AI-chip cycle made Habana a strategically essential acquisition rather than a commodity target.

name: Astera Labs
summary: Data-center connectivity chip maker IPO'd on Nasdaq in March 2024, popping over 70% on debut as AI infrastructure demand for its interconnect chips surged.
lesson: Riding the AI infrastructure buildout as a picks-and-shovels connectivity supplier let Astera capture value without competing directly with Nvidia.

name: Cerebras Systems
summary: Maker of the wafer-scale engine, the largest single AI training chip in the industry, has continued raising large funding rounds and signing major cloud and government AI compute contracts.
lesson: A genuinely novel architecture can carve out a real niche in AI training even against Nvidia's dominance, if backed by patient capital.

name: Groq
summary: Maker of the LPU inference chip has raised successive large funding rounds at rapidly rising valuations, driven by demand for fast, low-latency LLM inference.
lesson: Specializing narrowly in inference speed for large language models found a fast-growing niche Nvidia was not optimized for.

name: Nuvia
summary: Server CPU startup founded by ex-Apple chip architects was acquired by Qualcomm in 2021 for $1.4 billion, becoming the basis of Qualcomm's Snapdragon X and Oryon CPU cores.
lesson: A team with a proven track record designing category-leading mobile CPUs can command a premium acquisition even pre-product.

name: Ampere Computing
summary: Cloud-native Arm server processor designer, backed by Carlyle and Oracle, was acquired by SoftBank in a $6.5 billion all-cash deal that completed in late 2025.
lesson: Focusing purely on power-efficient cloud server chips built enough enterprise credibility to command a multi-billion-dollar exit.
